title: [InstCombine] Reuse with.overflow result for matching add/sub
author: JiangNingHX
description:
Reuse the result value of a dominating `llvm.[us]{add,sub}.with.overflow`
  when a later `add` or `sub` recomputes the same operands.

  Plain wrapping `add`/`sub` can always use `extractvalue 0`. For `nsw` or
  `nuw`, require the matching signed or unsigned overflow intrinsic and a
  dominating condition proving the overflow bit is false.

  This fixes the AArch64 case where recomputing `a + b` after
  `__builtin_add_overflow(a, b, &sum)` could leave an extra normal `add`
  after the flag-setting `adds`.

  Fixes #197671

tests.test_body:
declare void @use.i1(i1)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.sadd.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32) #0

define i32 @sadd_plain_recompute(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %pair = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.sadd.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %overflow = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %pair, 1
  call void @use.i1(i1 %overflow)
  %sum = add i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %sum
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

declare void @use.i1(i1)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.sadd.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32) #0

define i32 @sadd_nsw_unguarded_negative(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %pair = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.sadd.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %overflow = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %pair, 1
  call void @use.i1(i1 %overflow)
  %sum = add nsw i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %sum
}

}

define i32 @usub_nuw_guarded(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %pair = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.usub.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %builtin.diff = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %pair, 0
  %overflow = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %pair, 1
  call void @use.i32(i32 %builtin.diff)
  br i1 %overflow, label %overflowed, label %ok

ok:                                               ; preds = %entry
  %diff = sub nuw i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %diff

overflowed:                                       ; preds = %entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @use.i1(i1)

; Function Attrs: nocallback nocreateundeforpoison nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32) #0

define i32 @non_dominating_negative(i1 %c, i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %with.overflow, label %without.overflow

with.overflow:                                    ; preds = %entry
  %pair = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.i32(i32 %a, i32 %b)
  %overflow = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %pair, 1
  call void @use.i1(i1 %overflow)
  br label %merge

without.overflow:                                 ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %without.overflow, %with.overflow
  %sum = add i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %sum
}

check.signed:                                     ; preds = %entry

  %soverflow = extractvalue { i32, i1 } %spair, 1

ok:                                               ; preds = %check.signed

  ret i32 %sum

overflowed:                                       ; preds = %check.signed, %entry
  ret i32 0
}

comments.author: llvmorg-github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM PR SUMMARY COMMENT-->

@llvm/pr-subscribers-llvm-transforms

Author: Jiang Ning (JiangNingHX)

<details>
<summary>Changes</summary>

  Reuse the result value of a dominating `llvm.[us]{add,sub}.with.overflow`
  when a later `add` or `sub` recomputes the same operands.

  Plain wrapping `add`/`sub` can always use `extractvalue 0`. For `nsw` or
  `nuw`, require the matching signed or unsigned overflow intrinsic and a
  dominating condition proving the overflow bit is false.

  This fixes the AArch64 case where recomputing `a + b` after
  `__builtin_add_overflow(a, b, &sum)` could leave an extra normal `add`
  after the flag-setting `adds`.

  Fixes #<!-- -->197671

---

Patch is 22.25 KiB, truncated to 20.00 KiB below, full version: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/198992.diff


2 Files Affected:

- (modified) llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp (+109) 
- (added) llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/with-overflow-recompute.ll (+448) 


``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp
index 8c0dcc8029a1e..f95bd3466e476 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineAddSub.cpp
@@ -19,6 +19,7 @@
 #include "llvm/Analysis/ValueTracking.h"
 #include "llvm/IR/Constant.h"
 #include "llvm/IR/Constants.h"
+#include "llvm/IR/Dominators.h"
 #include "llvm/IR/InstrTypes.h"
 #include "llvm/IR/Instruction.h"
 #include "llvm/IR/Instructions.h"
@@ -31,6 +32,7 @@
 #include "llvm/Support/KnownBits.h"
 #include "llvm/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombiner.h"
 #include <cassert>
+#include <optional>
 #include <utility>
 
 using namespace llvm;
@@ -853,6 +855,107 @@ static Instruction *foldNoWrapAdd(BinaryOperator &Add,
   return nullptr;
 }
 
+static constexpr unsigned MaxOverflowUsersToScan = 32;
+
+static unsigned countUsersUpTo(const Value *V, unsigned MaxUsers) {
+  unsigned NumUsers = 0;
+  for (auto UI = V->use_begin(), UE = V->use_end(); UI != UE; ++UI)
+    if (++NumUsers > MaxUsers)
+      break;
+  return NumUsers;
+}
+
+static bool matchOverflowIntrinsicOperands(WithOverflowInst *WO,
+                                           BinaryOperator &I) {
+  Value *Op0 = I.getOperand(0);
+  Value *Op1 = I.getOperand(1);
+  if (WO->getLHS() == Op0 && WO->getRHS() == Op1)
+    return true;
+
+  return I.getOpcode() == Instruction::Add && WO->getLHS() == Op1 &&
+         WO->getRHS() == Op0;
+}
+
+static ExtractValueInst *findDominatingExtractValue(WithOverflowInst *WO,
+                                                    unsigned Index,
+                                                    BinaryOperator &I,
+                                                    DominatorTree &DT) {
+  unsigned ScannedUsers = 0;
+  for (User *U : WO->users()) {
+    if (ScannedUsers++ == MaxOverflowUsersToScan)
+      break;
+
+    auto *EV = dyn_cast<ExtractValueInst>(U);
+    if (!EV || EV->getNumIndices() != 1 || *EV->idx_begin() != Index)
+      continue;
+    if (DT.dominates(EV, &I))
+      return EV;
+  }
+  return nullptr;
+}
+
+static bool isNoWrapResultFromOverflowIntrinsicValid(WithOverflowInst *WO,
+                                                     BinaryOperator &I,
+                                                     DominatorTree &DT,
+                                                     const DataLayout &DL) {
+  bool HasNSW = I.hasNoSignedWrap();
+  bool HasNUW = I.hasNoUnsignedWrap();
+  if (!HasNSW && !HasNUW)
+    return true;
+
+  // Keep the first fold conservative: do not use one overflow check to justify
+  // both signed and unsigned no-wrap flags.
+  if (HasNSW && HasNUW)
+    return false;
+
+  if ((HasNSW && !WO->isSigned()) || (HasNUW && WO->isSigned()))
+    return false;
+
+  ExtractValueInst *Overflow =
+      findDominatingExtractValue(WO, /*Index=*/1, I, DT);
+  if (!Overflow)
+    return false;
+
+  std::optional<bool> Implied = isImpliedByDomCondition(Overflow, &I, DL);
+  return Implied && !*Implied;
+}
+
+static Instruction *reuseOverflowIntrinsicResult(BinaryOperator &I,
+                                                 InstCombinerImpl &IC) {
+  if (I.use_empty())
+    return nullptr;
+
+  Value *Op0 = I.getOperand(0);
+  Value *Op1 = I.getOperand(1);
+  unsigned Op0Users = countUsersUpTo(Op0, MaxOverflowUsersToScan);
+  unsigned Op1Users = countUsersUpTo(Op1, MaxOverflowUsersToScan);
+  Value *ScanOp = Op0Users <= Op1Users ? Op0 : Op1;
+
+  DominatorTree &DT = IC.getDominatorTree();
+  unsigned ScannedUsers = 0;
+  for (User *U : ScanOp->users()) {
+    if (ScannedUsers++ == MaxOverflowUsersToScan)
+      break;
+
+    auto *WO = dyn_cast<WithOverflowInst>(U);
+    if (!WO || WO->getBinaryOp() != I.getOpcode() ||
+        !matchOverflowIntrinsicOperands(WO, I) || !DT.dominates(WO, &I) ||
+        !isNoWrapResultFromOverflowIntrinsicValid(WO, I, DT,
+                                                  IC.getDataLayout()))
+      continue;
+
+    Value *Result = findDominatingExtractValue(WO, /*Index=*/0, I, DT);
+    if (!Result)
+      Result = IC.InsertNewInstWith(
+          ExtractValueInst::Create(WO, /*Idxs=*/0, "with.overflow.result"),
+          I.getIterator());
+
+    return IC.replaceInstUsesWith(I, Result);
+  }
+
+  return nullptr;
+}
+
 Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::foldAddWithConstant(BinaryOperator &Add) {
   Value *Op0 = Add.getOperand(0), *Op1 = Add.getOperand(1);
   Type *Ty = Add.getType();
@@ -1593,6 +1696,9 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitAdd(BinaryOperator &I) {
                                  SQ.getWithInstruction(&I)))
     return replaceInstUsesWith(I, V);
 
+  if (Instruction *R = reuseOverflowIntrinsicResult(I, *this))
+    return R;
+
   if (SimplifyAssociativeOrCommutative(I))
     return &I;
 
@@ -2369,6 +2475,9 @@ Instruction *InstCombinerImpl::visitSub(BinaryOperator &I) {
                                  SQ.getWithInstruction(&I)))
     return replaceInstUsesWith(I, V);
 
+  if (Instruction *R = reuseOverflowIntrinsicResult(I, *this))
+    return R;
+
   if (Instruction *X = foldVectorBinop(I))
     return X;
 
diff --git a/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/with-overflow-recompute.ll b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/with-overflow-recompute.ll
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000..90756db83f603
--- /dev/null
+++ b/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/with-overflow-recompute.ll
@@ -0,0 +1,448 @@
+; NOTE: Assertions have been autogenerated by utils/update_test_checks.py UTC_ARGS: --version 6
+; RUN: opt < %s -passes=instcombine -S | FileCheck %s
+
+declare void @use.i1(i1)
+declare void @use.i32(i32)
+declare void @use.v4i1(<4 x i1>)
+
+declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32)
+declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.sadd.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32)
+declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.usub.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32)
+declare { i32, i1 } @llvm.ssub.with.overflow.i32(i32, i32)
+declare { i64, i1 } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.i64(i64, i64)
+declare { <4 x i32>, <4 x i1> } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.v4i32(<4 x i32>, <4 x i32>)
+
+define i32 @uadd_plain_recompute(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
+; CHECK-LABEL: define i32 @uadd_plain_recompute(
+; CHECK-SAME: i32 [[A:%.*]], i32 [[B:%.*]]) {
+; CHECK-NEXT:  [[ENTRY:.*:]]
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[PAIR:%.*]] = call { i32, i1 } @llvm.uadd.with.overflow.i32(i32 [[A]], i32 [[B]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[BUILTIN_SUM:%.*]] = extractvalue { i32, i1 } [[PAIR]], 0
+; CHECK-NEXT:    [[OVERFLOW:%.*]] = extractvalue { i32, i1 } [[PAIR]], 1
+; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use.i32(i32 [[BUILTIN_SUM]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use.i1(i1 [[OVERFLOW]])
+; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[BUILTIN_SUM]]
+;
+entry:
``````````
